title: water — submerged ecosystems, pools, rain, the fluid register
body:
# water — submerged ecosystems, pools, rain, the fluid register

The single longest-running register in Colin's catalog. Water threads from
the earliest 07_08 alphabets sessions (`first otter water`, `bodyocean`,
`Sperm Whale`) all the way through the polished Killd By era
(`__gong-water`, `_river__in_side`, `SWIM (someone who isn't me)`,
`tsunami`). Across roughly a decade and several project aliases, water is
the most reliable Colin-coded environment.

Distinct from the **[[series/underwaters]]** series — that's six discrete
albums with a unified aquatic mythology. This motif page is the **broader
register**: every track across the catalog where water is the setting, the
material, the metaphor, or the pun. Many of these tracks belong to the
underwaters series; many do not.

## Tracks / coinages (selection)

### The underwaters series — the dense core

The series page ([[series/underwaters]]) lists all six volumes' track
listings. Standout titles for motif purposes:

- `underwaters/01 webbystreets.mp3` — the title that names the alphabets
  street-grid-as-tide-pool conceit.
- `underwaters II/06 lusty crawfish fuck hard.mp3`
- `underwaters II/robo whale growl swims.mp3`
- `underwaters III/01 glam fish bubble barf.mp3`
- `underwaters III/09 sad whale.mp3` — the emotional anchor of the series.
- `underwaters IV/03 whistle whales whisper tells.mp3`
- `underwaters IV/06 deepsea doctor operate.mp3`
- `underwaters V/02 deep creak speak.mp3`
- `underwaters V/06 sea sick.mp3` — closing note of the series; voyage as
  affliction.

### Killd By era — water makes it onto polished records

The motif survives the alphabets→Killd By transition and lands on Colin's
mature releases:

- `killd-by-court-clothes/12 __gong-water` — Court Clothes (2015) track 12.
  The motif on a finished album.
- `killd-by-court-clothes/13 _river__in_side` — Track 13 immediately
  follows. Two consecutive water tracks anchor the album's late side.
- `killd by - 2014-2016 -Bsides- - 01 SWIM (someone who isn't me)` — the
  B-sides compilation opens with water.
- `killd by - 2014-2016 -Bsides- - 09 rain HuLa.mp3`
- `B-SIDES2017/tsunami.aif`
- `killd by/Court Clothes/river in me (aac vers).m4a` — Court Clothes had a
  pre-release track called "river in me," later reframed as
  "_river__in_side." Same idea; water as interior territory.
- `RcvryDrafts/SWIM 22.aif` — Recovery-era continuation of SWIM.
- `SLOW&CUT/swim11 slow cut.mp3` — slowed-and-chopped variant.
- `SLOW/no memory SLOWswim.mp3`
- `SLOW/DjpOoLsiDe set#1take3 [underwaters edit].mp3` — explicit late-era
  callback to the alphabets-series. The DJ persona's set gets an
  "underwaters edit."

### DjPoolside — water as DJ-persona

Colin's DJ-set release name. The whole identity is water:

- `DJpoolside Vol.1`, `DjpOoLsiDe .1 [FuLLmiX]`, `Dj_pOoLsiDe 1`,
  `DjpOoLsiDe .1 - U.F.O. Flow` — multiple takes/typographies of the same
  set; preservation of the alternate spellings is part of the motif.
- `freakeasymix_NU-NU/poolside3&LonelyPPL.aif` — pool returns in the
  freakeasymix project.

### alphabets singles — water across the years

A roll across calendar-folder releases shows how steadily water comes back:

- `07_08 [_]/first otter water.m4a`
- `07_08 [_]/Sperm Whale.m4a`
- `07_08 [_]/bodyocean.m4a`
- `07_08 [_]/01 maybe seahorse.m4a`
- `07_08 [_]/06 Dusk Fish.m4a`
- `07_08 [_]/14 Sperm Whale.m4a`
- `07_08 [_]/DancePopDolphinIslands.m4a`
- `07_08 [_]/DolphinIslandsPart_2(dos).m4a`
- `07_08 [_]/Rooms, Spaces, Dolphins, Whales, Birds, Brainwaves, Joyous.m4a`
- `07_08 [_]/Starfish Breakfast.m4a`
- `07_08 [_]/Golfcourse_rivers_cunts_bulldozeroses.m4a`
- `09 [_]/swim it (fantasia mix).mp3`
- `09 [_]/swimmer or skier first.mp3`
- `09 [_]/different boomboxes droppd in pools.m4a`
- `09 [_]/the largest living animal on par with the largest animals that ever lived_blue whale.m4a`
- `09 [_]/bubblerings loop.m4a`
- `JANFEB09/bubbletrance__soccermatch.m4a`
- `JANFEB09/getting astounded by blue whales on videos.m4a`
- `M_A_Y_09[...]/03 maybe seal or otter.m4a`
- `M_A_Y_09[...]/07 melee_ rough seas angler fish lantern fights.m4a`
- `JUNE_MONSTERS_09/06 beach monster wave pump.m4a`
- `sep09/04 plesiosaur howl outs and swim bump.mp3`
- `sep09/07 jive shark and lulla fish.mp3`
- `BESTUV09/13 angler fish lantern fights.m4a`
- `samplebooks/starfish mobiles.mp3`
- `2010[_]/07 ocean swimmers.mp3`
- `2010[_]/09 river swimmers.mp3`
- `2010[_]/11 webbystreets underwater.mp3` — explicit "underwaters" callback.
- `2010[_]/11 beachy shells.mp3`
- `2010[_]/18 lake pears.mp3`
- `2010[_]/sad whales.mp3` — singular sad-whale flag preceding the
  underwaters III "sad whale" track.
- `JULY2010/07 sharky shirt.mp3`
- `OCTOBRR2010/04 dark w33d whales.mp3`
- `OCTOBRR2010/12 big wave stuff.mp3`
- `OCTOBRR2010/19 simba color autumn tide.mp3`
- `2011 [_]/sharkdraft.mp3`
- `2011 [_]/pool feet.mp3`
- `2011 [_]/floody.mp3`
- `2011 [_]/lifted all rainy weedy.mp3`
- `MAY2011DTE/05 happy new year aqua sports.mp3`
- `MAY2011DTE/06 cardio waterbaby.mp3`
- `instrumental/slow aqua.mp3`
- `instrumental/happy new year aqua sports.mp3`
- `fantazia2010/14 moving bear octopus.mp3`
- `fantazia2010/16 pond fish swim teams.mp3`
- `BIEBER JERBOA/07 (getting astounded by) blue whales on videos.mp3`
- `BIEBER JERBOA/02 brain waves dance hall.mp3` — wave as both ocean wave
  and brain wave.
- `DDR/02 fish _ jelly.mp3`, `DDR/03 turtle _ rainbow bee-eater.mp3`,
  `DDR/05 dolphin _ shark.mp3`, `DDR/06 seal _ sandpipers.mp3`,
  `DDR/crab-eater seal.mp3`, `DDR/dolphin.mp3`, `DDR/fish.mp3`,
  `DDR/shark.mp3` — the DDR series carries the water-creature inventory
  forward.
- `CAN U STAND THA RAIN/...` — release titled after rain.
- `_naturenature spring_summer 08/06 some sort of cool looking bird trapped in water and yelling for help.m4a`
- `NW wierd summer 08/dramdrams (deepsea jams).mp3`
- `NW wierd summer 08/otter's water.mp3`
- `alphabets accomp/oceangun120.m4a`
- `antPAD/01 oceangun.mp3`
- `TRIPLE PARROT/deep trunk when you hyper tank 1.aif`
- `SUNPOWER3/03 manajunk n floody.mp3`
- `may10/friend ships.mp3` — boats as friends, the friendship-as-flotilla
  pun.
- `soundtracks/forest green sea blue see blu e.mp3`
- `Other Projects/Colin Ward/backpack circle/05 more deeper than.mp3`
- `backpacksamples/more deeply.mp3`
- `400YEN/05 waver.mp3`
- `@PRILOSAURUS09/12 hot mess ankylosaurus drowning in a tar pit.m4a` —
  drowning as the verb-state.

### Rain — water from above

Rain is its own tight sub-cluster:

- `CAN U STAND THA RAIN` (release)
- `2010[_]/06 get all rainy.mp3`
- `2011 [_]/lifted all rainy weedy.mp3`
- `killd by - 2014-2016 -Bsides- - 09 rain HuLa.mp3`
- `2014-2016 Bsides/rainHulaPad.aif`
- `07_08 [_]/04 Rainbow Blanket Cuddle Puddle.m4a` and `08 Rainbow Quilt
  Cuddle Puddle.m4a` — rainbow as wet-aftermath of rain.
- `07_08 [_]/Rainclouds(icanfeelthem,youaretheuniverse).m4a`
- `freak medicine winter 07/05 (rainbow elvis.m4a` — the earliest documented
  rain reference.

## Reading

Four operations Colin reliably runs on water:

1. **The submerged ecosystem as character cast.** Underwaters I–V is the
   pure form: each track names a sea creature and gives it a domestic, pop,
   or emotional context (*lobster party*, *fashion fish dance clubs*,
   *seacow beauty contest*, *otter shaman dark magic potions*). The fish
   are not symbolic — they're the cast.
2. **Water as interior territory.** *_river_inside_*, *bodyocean*, *more
   deeper than*, *brain waves*. The body / mind contains a body of water.
   This operation is what carries the motif from alphabets-era cartoonery
   into Killd By's more inward register.
3. **Water as social register.** *DjPoolside* is the canonical move: the DJ
   persona is the pool, and "poolside" is where the listener sits. *swim
   teams*, *aqua sports*, *friend ships*, *blingbling mermaids* — water
   pulls in the language of leisure and sport.
4. **Rain as time-marker.** Rain pieces tend to land at the edges of
   sessions — *get all rainy* on the 2010 batch, *rain HuLa* late-era B-side.
   Rain marks weather changes, mood pivots; it's distinct from ocean as
   place.

The unifying logic across all four: water is **the wettest available
substance for whatever the song is about**. If a track is about feeling, it
gets a river inside. If it's about a creature, that creature lives at the
bottom of an ocean. If it's about a DJ, the DJ is at a pool.

## Notes for future passes

- The water motif **survives the alphabets → Killd By transition more
  cleanly than any other single motif**. This is the strongest evidence
  that Colin's late records are continuous with the early ones — not a
  break. Worth flagging on the website as a through-line that bridges the
  two project eras.
- *Sad whale* (track on underwaters III) and *sad whales* (singular track
  in 2010 [_]) suggest the same emotional figure recurs intentionally. May
  be worth a one-line note on each track entry.
- The `gong-water` / `river-inside` adjacency on Court Clothes (tracks 12
  and 13) is structural — Colin sequenced them next to each other on the
  album. Worth noting in the Court Clothes release page if it isn't
  already.
- DjPoolside identity has multiple folders with different typographies —
  `DJpoolside Vol.1`, `DjpOoLsiDe .1 [FuLLmiX]`, `Dj_pOoLsiDe 1`, etc. The
  underwaters edit (`SLOW/DjpOoLsiDe set#1take3 [underwaters edit].mp3`) is
  the explicit Killd-By-callback to the alphabets series. If a project
  page for DjPoolside doesn't exist yet, that edit is its load-bearing
  link.
- Sub-motifs not yet broken out: **rain as its own page**, **the bubble
  family** (`bubbles and vitamins`, `bubbletrance__soccermatch`,
  `bubblerings loop`, `glam fish bubble barf`), **deep / depth** (`deep
  creak speak`, `deepsea doctor operate`, `deepsea pegasus armies`, `more
  deeper than`, `more deeply`, `dark w33d whales`). Could each merit their
  own thinner motif page if the website wants finer-grained navigation.
- Pairs with **[[motifs/animal-coinage]]** (much of the underwaters cast is
  animal-coinage applied to aquatic species) and the partly-implied
  **kid/youth/play** motif still on the carry-forward queue (pool, swim
  teams, beachy shells).
